info.instructions:
• Task: Read the source text and competing translations. Highlight all errors. Rate each translation (each column is an output from a single model/translator). At the end, click Next in top-right corner.

  • Highlighting errors:
    • Select text containing an error to mark it as Minor.
    • Click selected text to change the error to Major or remove the highlight.
    • Missing text: Highlight the [MISSING] tag if the translation omits important source text.

  • Error severity:
    • Minor: Imperfections or stylistic issues that do not impact the core message (e.g., awkward phrasing).
    • Major: Confuses meaning, misrepresents the source, or violates the message (e.g., incorrect information, confusing wording).

  • Important rules:
    • Multiple errors: Use separate highlights for each error.
    • Hallucinations: Highlight unsupported extra text; mark as Major.
    • Wrong language: Highlight the entire text, mark as Major, and assign a score of 0.
    • Consistency: Check translation consistency (e.g., technical terms) across the document.

  • Rating scale (0-100%):
    • 85-100% (Very Good): Complete meaning transfer; perfectly natural; no or minimal proofreading.
    • 65-80% (Good): Near-complete transfer, minor inaccuracies; mostly natural, minor awkwardness; needs light proofreading.
    • 45-60% (Acceptable): Main ideas conveyed, noticeable inaccuracies or omissions; uneven naturalness, awkward phrasing; usable only after substantial revision.
    • 25-40% (Borderline): Partial transfer; frequent misinterpretation or omission confusing the message; often unnatural; requires major rewrite.
    • 0-20% (Not acceptable): Violation of meaning; large portions mistranslated, missing, or incoherent; unusable without complete retranslation.

  • For unexpected problems and remarks, use comment box in ⚙️ (top right).

payload.src_text: Sometimes we don't change anything. Usually we change one or two stories, so one of us will have to rewrite something, most likely. But this way we can kind of get ahead of it, start doing those newsfuse stories. And this is one of those days where we have four randomizers. It's going to be a big pull. Also, you can see sarah has gotten in and put in one of her discussion stories. I mean, you guys may think, like, there are such flockers, they haven't been in there. They're working outside of the dock a lot. You don't want to work in the dock. You notice that i didn't even work in the dock all that much. Because, oh, and i forgot to put my notes in there. Better do that before i'm forgetting it. Because the dock may crash, and you don't want to lose your stuff. So all this tells me is when they've really decided, like, oh yeah, i'm definitely going to do that story. So she's actually taking on...

tgt.CommandA-MT': 時々、何も変えないこともありますが、通常は1つか2つの記事を変更するので、誰かが書き直す必要があるでしょう。でも、この方法なら、ある程度先手を打って、ニュース記事の作成に取りかかることができます。そして今日は、4つのランダムなタスクがある日です。これはかなりの負担になるでしょう。また、サラが参加して、彼女のディスカッション記事を投稿したことがわかります。つまり、あなたたちは、ああいう人たちがいると思うかもしれませんが、彼らはそこにいません。彼らはドックの外でよく働いています。ドックで働くのは嫌ですよね。私もドックでほとんど働いていなかったことに気づいたでしょう。なぜなら、メモをそこに入れるのを忘れていたからです。忘れる前にやった方がいいですね。なぜなら、ドックがクラッシュする可能性があり、データを失いたくないからです。つまり、彼らが本当に決心したとき、つまり、絶対にその記事を書こうと思ったときが分かるということです。だから、彼女は実際にその記事に取り組んでいるのです…
